title: notodanthonia induta
intro: Notodanthonia induta, also known as the hooked danthonia, is a species of grass native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving lung function, making it a potential treatment for chronic respiratory conditions. Notodanthonia induta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory issues.
